documents.title: What is ABHA Card?
documents.content: ABHA stands for Ayushman Bharat Health Account. It is a 14-digit unique health ID for every Indian citizen. ABHA card stores all your health records digitally. It is part of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM). You can use ABHA to share health records with doctors and hospitals easily.

documents.title: How to create ABHA Card?
documents.content: To create ABHA card, visit the official website abdm.gov.in or use the ABHA app. You can register using your Aadhaar card or driving license or mobile number. Enter your Aadhaar number and verify with OTP sent to your mobile. Fill in personal details like name, date of birth, gender. Your 14-digit ABHA number will be generated instantly. You can download the ABHA card as PDF or image.

documents.title: How to download ABHA Card?
documents.content: To download ABHA card, go to abdm.gov.in or open the ABHA mobile app. Login using your ABHA number or mobile number with OTP verification. Go to My Profile section and click on Download Card. Your ABHA card will be downloaded as a PDF file. You can also download it from the AarogyaOne PHR app after linking your ABHA account.

documents.title: ABHA Card benefits
documents.content: ABHA card provides many benefits to patients. It gives a unique health identity to every citizen. All medical records prescriptions lab reports are stored digitally. Doctors can access your health history with your consent. It reduces need to carry physical documents to hospitals. ABHA is free of cost for all Indian citizens. It works across all hospitals and health facilities in India.

documents.title: What is Ayushman Bharat Card?
documents.content: Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana or PMJAY is a health insurance scheme. It provides health coverage of Rs 5 lakh per family per year. It covers poor and vulnerable families across India. Ayushman card is the golden card for accessing free treatment. Over 50 crore people are covered under this scheme. It covers pre and post hospitalization expenses.

documents.title: How to get Ayushman Card?
documents.content: To get Ayushman card first check your eligibility on pmjay.gov.in. Enter your mobile number and OTP to check if your family is eligible. Visit nearest Common Service Centre or Ayushman Mitra at empanelled hospital. Bring your Aadhaar card and ration card. Biometric verification will be done. Your Ayushman golden card will be issued on the spot or within a few days.

documents.title: How to download Ayushman Card?
documents.content: To download Ayushman Bharat card visit the official website pmjay.gov.in. Click on Am I Eligible to check your status first. Login to the beneficiary portal with your mobile number. Enter OTP for verification. Go to Download Card option. Enter your Ayushman card number or Aadhaar number. Your Ayushman card will be downloaded as PDF. You can also visit your nearest CSC centre for help.

documents.title: Ayushman Card benefits and coverage
documents.content: Ayushman Bharat card covers Rs 5 lakh health insurance per family per year. It covers over 1500 treatment procedures and surgeries. Coverage includes pre-hospitalization 3 days and post-hospitalization 15 days. It covers all pre-existing diseases from day one. There are over 25000 empanelled hospitals across India. Treatment is cashless at government and private hospitals. Covers ICU charges, medicines, surgery, diagnostic tests.

documents.title: What is COVID vaccination certificate?
documents.content: COVID-19 vaccination certificate is a digital proof of vaccination. It is issued by the Government of India through CoWIN platform. Certificate contains your name, date of birth, vaccine name, dose number, vaccination date, beneficiary ID. It is internationally recognized for travel purposes. Both partial vaccination one dose and full vaccination two doses certificates are issued.

documents.title: How to download COVID vaccination certificate?
documents.content: To download COVID certificate visit cowin.gov.in official website. Click on Register or Sign In option. Enter your registered mobile number and OTP. Your vaccination details will appear on screen. Click on Download Certificate button. Certificate will be downloaded as PDF. You can also download from UMANG app or DigiLocker app. Aarogya Setu app also provides certificate download option.

documents.title: COVID certificate lost or not received
documents.content: If you have not received your COVID certificate or lost it you can redownload it from CoWIN portal. Visit cowin.gov.in and login with your registered mobile number. If mobile number changed you can raise a correction request. Contact your vaccination center with your beneficiary ID. You can also contact the CoWIN helpline at 1075 for assistance. Certificate correction requests can be raised on the portal.

documents.title: What is AarogyaOne PHR app?
documents.content: AarogyaOne PHR is a Personal Health Record application. It is developed by JDeanZ Healthtech. The app helps you store and manage all your health records digitally. You can link your ABHA account with AarogyaOne. It is available on Google Play Store. AarogyaOne helps you maintain complete medical history in one place. It is ABDM compliant and follows government health data standards.

documents.title: How to upload documents in AarogyaOne?
documents.content: To upload documents in AarogyaOne app open the app on your phone. Login with your registered mobile number. Go to My Health Records section in the bottom menu. Tap on the plus button or Upload Document. Select document type like prescription, lab report, discharge summary. You can take a photo of the document or choose from gallery. Add document date and doctor name. Tap Save to upload the document to your health record.

documents.title: How to book appointment in AarogyaOne?
documents.content: To book appointment in AarogyaOne open the app and login. Go to Appointments section. Search for doctor by name, speciality, or hospital. Select your preferred doctor and check available time slots. Choose a convenient date and time slot. Confirm your appointment details. You will receive confirmation on your registered mobile. You can also view and cancel appointments from the app.

documents.title: How to link ABHA with AarogyaOne?
documents.content: To link ABHA account with AarogyaOne app open the app. Go to Profile or Settings section. Tap on Link ABHA Account option. Enter your 14 digit ABHA number. Verify using OTP sent to your registered mobile. Your ABHA account will be linked to AarogyaOne. After linking you can access all your health records from ABDM network. All records from AarogyaOne will also sync with your ABHA account.

documents.title: How to view health records in AarogyaOne?
documents.content: To view health records in AarogyaOne open the app and login. Go to My Health Records from home screen or bottom menu. You can see all uploaded documents prescriptions lab reports. Filter records by type date or doctor. Tap on any record to view it in full. You can share health records with doctors using consent mechanism. Records are stored securely and encrypted for your privacy.

documents.title: How to download AarogyaOne app?
documents.content: AarogyaOne PHR app is available on Google Play Store. Search for AarogyaOne in Play Store. Or use this link: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.jdeanzhealthtech.aarogyaone. Tap Install to download and install the app. Open the app and register with your mobile number. The app is free to download and use. It is compatible with Android phones.

documents.title: How to share records with doctor in AarogyaOne?
documents.content: To share health records with a doctor in AarogyaOne open the app. Go to My Health Records and select the record you want to share. Tap on Share button. Choose share via ABHA or share as PDF. For sharing via ABHA enter the doctor or facility ABHA address. Set consent duration for how long doctor can access. Doctor can view your records with your permission. You can revoke access anytime from Consent section.

documents.title: Common cold and fever symptoms
documents.content: Common cold symptoms include runny nose, sneezing, sore throat, mild fever, cough, congestion. Fever above 102 degrees Fahrenheit needs medical attention. Take rest and drink plenty of fluids. Paracetamol can help reduce fever. If symptoms persist beyond 7 days consult a doctor. COVID-19 can also present like cold symptoms so get tested if needed.

documents.title: Diabetes management tips
documents.content: Diabetes management requires regular blood sugar monitoring. Take prescribed medications regularly. Follow a healthy diet low in sugar and refined carbohydrates. Exercise for at least 30 minutes daily. Avoid smoking and alcohol. Regular doctor visits every 3 months. HbA1c test every 6 months. Monitor blood pressure regularly. Check feet daily for any wounds. Eye checkup annually for diabetics.

documents.title: High blood pressure hypertension advice
documents.content: High blood pressure or hypertension should be managed carefully. Take medicines regularly as prescribed by doctor. Reduce salt intake in your diet. Exercise regularly with walking or yoga. Maintain healthy weight. Avoid stress and practice meditation. Quit smoking immediately. Limit alcohol consumption. Monitor BP regularly at home. Normal BP is below 120 by 80. Above 140 by 90 is high BP.

documents.title: When to visit emergency doctor
documents.content: Visit emergency immediately for chest pain or pressure, difficulty breathing, severe headache, sudden vision loss, paralysis of face or limbs, high fever above 104 degrees, severe bleeding, loss of consciousness, seizures. These are signs of medical emergency. Do not delay treatment. Call 108 ambulance service in India for emergency. Inform family members during medical emergency.

documents.title: Health insurance and government schemes
documents.content: Government of India offers multiple health schemes. Ayushman Bharat PMJAY provides Rs 5 lakh insurance. ESIC provides health coverage for employees. Central Government Health Scheme for government employees. State governments have their own health schemes. Check eligibility on official government portals. Health insurance is important for financial protection during illness.

documents.title: National health helplines India
documents.content: Important health helpline numbers in India. National Health Helpline 104 for medical advice. Emergency Ambulance 108 available 24 hours. CoWIN helpline 1075 for COVID vaccination. Mental health helpline iCall 9152987821. Suicide prevention Vandrevala Foundation 1860-2662-345. NIMHANS helpline 080-46110007. These services are available in multiple Indian languages.
